nameMeanings.name: Mabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

en.lifeSummary: Mabel was born in 1275, in Edgefield, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Thomas Piers Roscelyn. She died in 1307, in her hometown, at the age of 32.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabelは1275年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクエジェフィールドで生まれた。彼女はThomas Piers Roscelynとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1307年に故郷で32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabel(은)는 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 에지필드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Piers Roscelyn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1307년 그녀의 고향에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.
